one_time_description: Settlement revenue of $5,961,353 recognized in 2021 (vs $1,662,735 in 2020), related to court-ordered settlement between Piikani Nation and its subsidiaries including Piikani Resource Development Ltd. and the Settlement Trust
economic_notes: Nation holds 25% interest in Oldman River Hydro Joint Venture and 50.995% interest in PiikaniLink Limited Partnership (electricity transmission). Travel Plaza (fuel/convenience) opened during year. COVID-19 pandemic noted as impacting operations. Community Futures Treaty 7 involvement noted.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Net financial debt position of $26,228,283. 13 consolidated subsidiaries including housing corporation, board of education, resource development, travel plaza, investment corporation and several numbered companies. 2 GBEs accounted for by equity method: Oldman River Hydro Joint Venture (25%) and PiikaniLink Limited Partnership (50.995%). Large increase in deferred revenue from $17.3M to $25.5M suggesting significant unspent funding. Settlement revenue of $5.96M is materially higher than prior year $1.66M. Long-term debt of $50.75M is substantial. Revenue figures shown are net of deferred revenue adjustment; gross revenue before deferred revenue adjustment was $64,601,399. ISC funding shown is gross as reported on face of statement before deferred revenue netting. The statement shows net revenue of $56,326,873 after deferred revenue adjustment of -$8,274,526. Total government transfers may include provincial and other federal sources beyond ISC captured in 'Other' revenue line - exact breakdown not fully available. Chief and council honorariums of $1,215,396 disclosed as separate line item. Piikani Travel Plaza Ltd. appears to be a new entity with $4.5M+ in new debt. Bankers acceptance of ~$9.6M to TD Bank for transmission partnership.
